title: Fix CTkEntry place_holder
body:
This PR focuses on stabilizing lazy placeholder behavior when a textvariable is bound to CTkEntry and introducing a flag to control the textvariable callback event. The main goal is to prevent the textvariable trace from interfering with placeholder activation/deactivation and to make the placeholder lifecycle predictable when external code updates the bound variable.

Key ideas:
- Introduce a control flag for the textvariable callback so that programmatic updates (e.g., placeholder toggling) don’t recursively trigger placeholder logic.
- Guard the placeholder activation/deactivation routines (activate placeholder / deactivate placeholder) with temporary suppression of the textvariable callback when the code itself is manipulating the Entry content.
- Maintain existing API and behavior for normal usage, while making the internal placeholder/textvariable interplay safer and more deterministic.

Impact and scope:
- Targeted changes live in the CTkEntry implementation used by CustomTkinter (Windows variant in this repository).
- No breaking changes to public API; a new internal flag (and a setter) is added to control the behavior, with optional public accessor to toggle if needed later.
- Tests should cover: placeholder shown when textvariable becomes empty, placeholder hidden when user types, and that programmatic updates to the textvariable don’t inadvertently re-activate the placeholder during internal updates.

Files touched (summary):
- customtkinter/windows/widgets/ctk_entry.py
  - CTkEntry.__init__: initialize new textvariable-callback-control flag and prepare for guarded updates.
  - CTkEntry._textvariable_callback: honor the control flag to avoid undesired placeholder toggling during external textvariable changes.
  - CTkEntry._activate_placeholder / _deactivate_placeholder: guard changes with the control flag to prevent triggering callback loops when the widget itself updates the Entry content for placeholder handling.
  - New CTkEntry.set_textvariable_callback_enabled(enabled: bool) (optional public setter): enables/disables the textvariable callback globally for this widget instance.
  - Optional: ensure any temporary disabling of the callback during placeholder transitions is clearly scoped and restored.

Expected outcome:
- Placeholder behavior remains correct when a textvariable is bound, even if external code manipulates the textvariable.
- No accidental re-entry or infinite loops caused by the trace callback during placeholder activation/deactivation.
- A simple API to control the textvariable callback flow, enabling safer placeholder transitions in complex UI flows.

step_plan.summary: This PR stabilizes CTkEntry placeholder behavior when a textvariable is bound by adding a guard flag for the textvariable callback and guarding placeholder activation/deactivation updates, plus a public setter to control the callback flow. The changes prevent recursive or unintended placeholder logic during internal updates while preserving existing API and behavior.

steps.operation: Initialize internal textvariable callback guard flag
steps.reason: Introduce a per-instance flag (e.g., _textvariable_callback_enabled) defaulting to True to preserve existing behavior while allowing temporary suppression during internal updates.
steps.side_effects: Adds a new attribute during initialization; minor impact on object creation and potential tests to verify default state.

steps.reason: During external textvariable updates, only react to changes when the guard flag is enabled; prevents placeholder logic from running during internal updates.
steps.side_effects: External code that toggles the flag affects callback behavior; ensure compatibility with existing bindings and that the flag is respected consistently.

steps.operation: Guard placeholder activation with temporary suppression of the textvariable callback
steps.reason: When inserting placeholder text programmatically, disable the textvariable callback to avoid triggering placeholder logic or recursion.
steps.side_effects: Restore callback state after activation; ensure exception safety to avoid leaving the callback disabled unintentionally.

steps.operation: Guard placeholder deactivation with temporary suppression of the textvariable callback
steps.reason: During removal of placeholder text, ensure the internal text updates do not trigger the textvariable trace.
steps.side_effects: Restore callback state after deactivation; handle errors to prevent leaving the callback suppressed.

steps.operation: Add public API to enable/disable the textvariable callback
steps.reason: Provide external control to tests and advanced users to disable the trace during complex placeholder transitions or batch updates.
steps.side_effects: Introduces a new public API; default behavior remains enabled; may require tests/docs to cover usage and ensure backward compatibility.
